reasoning: Both models correctly identified the PKGBUILD uses secure practices: pinned GitHub release with valid checksum, no dangerous commands in build/install phases. Binary tarball analysis is inherently limited but standard for AUR audits.
coverage_issues: Both models skipped binary tarball analysis - critical gap for -bin packages where prebuilt binaries are the primary payload
false_negatives: Potential binary payload risks (malware/backdoors) in kdash-bin-2.0.1.tar.gz were not analyzed due to binary skipping
learnings: Prioritize binary/artifact analysis for -bin packages using basic static analysis tools
Improve file selection logic to flag binary files as critical for -bin packages
Add warnings about binary analysis limitations in reports
Standardize checks for GitHub release provenance validation beyond checksums
